item.title: Precious Metal 3D Printing: Gold, Platinum, and Silver SLM Production
item.description: Selective laser melting of precious metals enables geometry impossible in lost-wax casting. This guide covers alloy compositions, process parameters, and design guidelines for gold and platinum printing.

item.title: About Gold 3D Print
item.description:
Where Precious Metal Meets Additive Manufacturing

Gold 3D Print covers direct precious metal 3D printing — selective laser melting of gold, platinum, and silver powders to produce jewelry and luxury objects with geometry that lost-wax casting cannot achieve.

The technology enables: hollow forms that preserve visual volume while reducing metal content by 50–70%, interlocking mechanisms that print as a single assembled piece, filigree to 0.1 mm wall thickness, and personalized geometry from CAD files in 5–7 days.

item.title: Advertise on Gold 3D Print
item.description:
Advertise on Gold 3D Print

Gold 3D Print reaches engineers and procurement managers actively sourcing metal additive manufacturing services, materials, and equipment.

Advertising Packages

Package Rate Placement
Banner Ad (728×90) $500/month Homepage
Sponsored Content $1,200/month Article feature
Network Package $3,500/month All PrintWire metal sites

Contact: maxdavisgroup@gmail.com

item.title: Services
item.description:
Gold & Platinum Printing

Precious metal SLM printing bypasses lost-wax casting to produce jewelry directly from CAD — hollow forms, interlocking mechanisms, and filigree geometries that casting cannot achieve. 18K gold alloys (yellow, white, rose) in 15–25 µm particle size; 950 platinum; 999 Ag fine silver. All compositions are hallmark-compliant. Minimum feature: 0.3 mm wall thickness, 0.1 mm resolution on fine detail.

Custom Jewelry Production

Production jewelry via precious metal SLM: hollow pendants that reduce gold weight 50–70% while maintaining volume, interlocking ring-chain systems that print as assembled, pavé lattices for diamond setting in complex undercut geometries, and personalized items with unique identifiers printed directly. CAD-to-finished-piece in 5–7 days for one-of-a-kind work; 2–3 weeks for batch production.
